page: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server instance designed for intensive computational workloads. It features four NVIDIA H100 GPUs from the Hopper architecture, providing a total of 319 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 104 vCPUs (52 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 936.0 GB of system memory, translating to 9.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across multiple domains. The CPU achieved a top-tier rating in the Passmark CPU score and the Passmark CPU encryption test. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests both placed in the top 25%. In memory operations, the instance demonstrated strong read-only small block bandwidth at 6842.06 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, Geekbench image processing scored in the top 10%, while LLM text generation using a medium 7B model reached 38.11 tokens per second.

Given the integration of high-density hardware including four enterprise-grade GPUs, a large memory footprint, and local NVMe storage, this instance represents a high-resource tier. The qualitative cost efficiency is optimized for workloads that can fully utilize parallel GPU processing and high memory bandwidth, offsetting the premium hardware class. The lack of complimentary public IPv4 addresses requires external networking configuration. This server is suited for large-scale machine learning training, LLM inference, high-performance computing, data science, and database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for high-performance computing and machine learning workloads. It is equipped with four NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs offering 319 GB of total VRAM, alongside 104 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system features 936.0 GB of RAM and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in Passmark CPU and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The instance is highly capable in LLM text generation, achieving 38.11 tokens per second on a 7B model. This high-density hardware profile is optimized for parallel processing, making it suitable for deep learning, large-scale data science, and intensive database operations where GPU acceleration and high memory capacity are critical.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g features 4 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 104 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 936GB RAM, and 3216GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier performance for machine learning and HPC workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g is a GPU-accelerated server with 4 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 104 vCPUs, and 936GB RAM for machine learning workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring four high-performance GPUs and massive memory capacity for demanding machine learning and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs with 319 GB total VRAM
104 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 936 GB of system memory
3216 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in CPU benchmarks and image processing
Optimized for LLM inference, machine learning training, and HPC
